methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy, a Series D climate and energy company founded by Kunal Girotra, has raised over $300M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies and 1 public peer (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage cohort.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $300M total raised as a proxy for recent valuation, applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $450M. Peer-median analysis examined four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Redwood Materials at $6.0B (Series D), KoBold Metals at $2.0B (Series C), Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B (Series C), and Amogy at $700M (Series C). The median across this peer set is $1.5B, with a range of $700M to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a low bound of $382M, a median of $975M, and a high bound of $1.7B. The wide dispersion—particularly driven by Redwood Materials' $6.0B outlier valuation—reflects material uncertainty in how market participants price early-stage energy infrastructure at this stage. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to peers, path to revenue, and capital intensity of scaling. The median range of $975M represents the central tendency absent additional company-specific operational or market data.
